# CrowdStrike Falcon Events Data Dictionary

> Open, machine-readable reference of CrowdStrike Falcon sensor events, exported
> from the official (auth-gated, 1,500+ page) PDF into clean CSV and Markdown.
> Designed for threat hunting, detection engineering, SIEM onboarding, and
> AI/LLM retrieval (RAG). The current snapshot documents 998 events across
> Windows, Linux, macOS, iOS, Android, ChromeOS, Falcon Container, Kubernetes,
> Public Cloud, and Forensics platforms.

Each record contains: the event name (header), a human-readable description,
and the list of supported platforms. Snapshots are stored in ISO-dated folders
so the schema can be diffed across releases.

## Data (latest: 2026-06-02)

- [Sensor events (Markdown)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erickatwork/CrowdStrike-Falcon-Events-Data-Dictionary/main/data/2026-06-02/sensor_events.md): All 998 events with descriptions and platforms; best for LLM context windows and RAG.
- [Sensor events (CSV)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erickatwork/CrowdStrike-Falcon-Events-Data-Dictionary/main/data/2026-06-02/sensor_events.csv): Columns header,description,platforms; best for dataframes, SQL, and embeddings.

## Previous snapshots

- [2025-10-24 (CSV)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erickatwork/CrowdStrike-Falcon-Events-Data-Dictionary/main/data/2025-10-24/sensor_events.csv): Earlier export for diffing schema changes over time.

## Tools

- [extract_events.py](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erickatwork/CrowdStrike-Falcon-Events-Data-Dictionary/main/app/extract_events.py): Converts the reference PDF into CSV and Markdown.
- [examine_pdf.py](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erickatwork/CrowdStrike-Falcon-Events-Data-Dictionary/main/app/examine_pdf.py): Inspects and searches the raw PDF.

## Source

- [Official CrowdStrike sensor events documentation](https://docs.crowdstrike.com/r/en-US/sensormap.ftmap): Authoritative source; treat as source of truth.

## Notes

- Unofficial, community-maintained project. Not affiliated with or endorsed by CrowdStrike, Inc.
- "CrowdStrike" and "Falcon" are trademarks of CrowdStrike, Inc.
- Code is Apache-2.0 licensed; event definitions remain the property of CrowdStrike.
